_doc: STQ-02 refill-guard variant (different field-combo from Plan 04-04's F21934D4 to avoid duplicate coverage). Inspector moves on: 'Move on to circuit three, Zs nought point two five.' — supply-level Ze (circuit 1, 0.32) is already on the snapshot from an earlier turn, and neither path should re-emit / re-ask it. Legacy emits record_extraction with only the new circuit 3 zs reading; tool-call emits only record_reading(3,zs,'0.25'). Both converge on circuits[3].zs='0.25' while circuit 1's ze remains untouched. Because the normaliser only looks at the EXTRACTION RESULT (not the final snapshot), 'circuit 1 ze unchanged' is visible as the absence of a (1,ze) reading in either output — NOT as a positive slot equal to '0.32'.
_why_distinct_from_F21934D4: F21934D4 locks the r1_r2-on-circuit-2 pre-fill scenario (STA-02 / STQ-05 — the specific pair that triggered the production re-ask). sample-05 here locks Ze-on-circuit-1 as a supply-level-value pre-fill. Different field, different circuit, same RESTRAINT principle. Covering both shapes proves the guard is not field-specific.
_requirement: STT-11 + STQ-05 (restraint — do not re-emit filled slots). STT-11 golden-session subset entry.
transcript: Move on to circuit three, Zs nought point two five.

sse_events_legacy._comment: Legacy under post-Phase-4 prompt — note the empty questions_for_user (Plan 04-03 deletion) and the ABSENCE of any re-emission for circuit 1 ze. Only the new reading for circuit 3 zs appears.

sse_events_tool_call._comment: Round 1 — one record_reading tool_use for the new reading. No ask_user, no additional writes to circuit 1, no clear_reading on the pre-filled ze.

expected_slot_writes._doc: Circuit 1 ze is UNCHANGED — expected_slot_writes describes only what the turn wrote, not the full post-turn snapshot. The RESTRAINT signal is the ABSENCE of (1,ze) in both paths' extracted_readings.
